findings.description: The signature/facility information section at the bottom of the page loses its two-column layout. In the PDF, the NC logo sits to the left of the form fields (TITLE or POSITION through EMAIL). In the HTML, this two-column arrangement is collapsed to a single column, causing the logo to appear between PRINTED NAME and TITLE or POSITION, interrupting the form field sequence.
findings.evidence: PDF: NC logo occupies the left column alongside TITLE or POSITION, FACILITY ADDRESS, FACILITY CITY & ZIP, FACILITY PHONE, and EMAIL fields on the right. HTML: NC logo is rendered full-width between PRINTED NAME and TITLE or POSITION, breaking the logical grouping of form fields.

findings.description: Reading order of the signature section is disrupted by the NC logo being placed inline between form fields rather than alongside them.
findings.evidence: PDF reading order: DATE, SIGNATURE, PRINTED NAME, TITLE or POSITION, FACILITY ADDRESS, FACILITY CITY & ZIP, FACILITY PHONE, EMAIL (with logo as a side element). HTML reading order: DATE, SIGNATURE, PRINTED NAME, [NC logo + text], TITLE or POSITION, FACILITY ADDRESS, FACILITY CITY & ZIP, FACILITY PHONE, EMAIL. The logo interrupts the natural form-field sequence.
